verses.text: The heir, while a child, 'differeth nothing from a servant'; so we were 'in bondage under the elements.' Under tutors. Before Christ we were as minors.
The heir, while a child, 'differeth nothing from a servant'; so we were 'in bondage under the elements.' Under tutors. Before Christ we were as minors.
The heir, while a child, 'differeth nothing from a servant'; so we were 'in bondage under the elements.' Under tutors. Before Christ we were as minors.
'When the fulness of the time was come, God sent forth his Son... that we might receive the adoption of sons.' At the right time. Christ came to make us sons.
'When the fulness of the time was come, God sent forth his Son... that we might receive the adoption of sons.' At the right time. Christ came to make us sons.
God sent 'the Spirit of his Son... crying, Abba, Father'; thou art 'no more a servant, but a son.' Adopted. The Spirit makes us cry to God as Father.
God sent 'the Spirit of his Son... crying, Abba, Father'; thou art 'no more a servant, but a son.' Adopted. The Spirit makes us cry to God as Father.
Ye knew not God, yet turn again to 'weak and beggarly elements'; I fear for you. Backward step. To return to law is to forsake grace.
Ye knew not God, yet turn again to 'weak and beggarly elements'; I fear for you. Backward step. To return to law is to forsake grace.
Ye knew not God, yet turn again to 'weak and beggarly elements'; I fear for you. Backward step. To return to law is to forsake grace.
Ye knew not God, yet turn again to 'weak and beggarly elements'; I fear for you. Backward step. To return to law is to forsake grace.
Ye received me 'as an angel of God'; 'where is then the blessedness ye spake of?' Faded love. Their first joy in him had cooled.
Ye received me 'as an angel of God'; 'where is then the blessedness ye spake of?' Faded love. Their first joy in him had cooled.
Ye received me 'as an angel of God'; 'where is then the blessedness ye spake of?' Faded love. Their first joy in him had cooled.

Ye received me 'as an angel of God'; 'where is then the blessedness ye spake of?' Faded love. Their first joy in him had cooled.
They zealously court you, 'but not well,' that ye might court them. False zeal. The legalists sought their own following.
They zealously court you, 'but not well,' that ye might court them. False zeal. The legalists sought their own following.
My little children, 'of whom I travail in birth again until Christ be formed in you.' A mother's pain. He labours till Christ is shaped in them.
My little children, 'of whom I travail in birth again until Christ be formed in you.' A mother's pain. He labours till Christ is shaped in them.
Abraham had two sons, one by a bondwoman, one 'by promise.' Two births. The one of the flesh, the other of grace.
Abraham had two sons, one by a bondwoman, one 'by promise.' Two births. The one of the flesh, the other of grace.
Abraham had two sons, one by a bondwoman, one 'by promise.' Two births. The one of the flesh, the other of grace.
These are an allegory of two covenants; 'Jerusalem which is above is free, which is the mother of us all.' Free city. The covenant of grace bears free children.
These are an allegory of two covenants; 'Jerusalem which is above is free, which is the mother of us all.' Free city. The covenant of grace bears free children.
These are an allegory of two covenants; 'Jerusalem which is above is free, which is the mother of us all.' Free city. The covenant of grace bears free children.
These are an allegory of two covenants; 'Jerusalem which is above is free, which is the mother of us all.' Free city. The covenant of grace bears free children.
We are 'the children of promise'; 'cast out the bondwoman'; we are children of the free. Liberty. The free-born inherit, not the bond.
